title: Decisions, Pattern Matching, and Iteration · Independent Program Trial
summary: Route support requests through priority rules, repeated records, and changed data conditions. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a support-request routing engine with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Predict truth testing, chained comparisons, equality, identity, membership, and Boolean operator results.
Use if, elif, else, conditional expressions, and guard clauses to express exclusive and early-exit decisions.
Use match and case for structural patterns, captures, guards, alternatives, and exhaustive fallback behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
